title: Indulgent Icy Vietnamese Ackee Bun Bo Hue
description: A creamy, malty, and dense Vietnamese one-pot dish featuring ackee, perfect for a cold winter day. This egg-free-friendly version of the classic Bun Bo Hue is sure to become a favorite.
instructions: In a large hot pot, combine chicken broth, fish sauce, soy sauce, sugar, and minced ginger. Bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together.
Add the coconut milk and stir to combine.
Cook for an additional 5 minutes, or until the mixture has thickened slightly.
Add the ackee and stir to combine.
Cook for 2-3 minutes, or until the ackee has heated through.
Add the rice noodles to the pot and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until they have softened.
Stir in the chopped cilantro and lime juice.
Serve the Bun Bo Hue hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
Let each guest assemble their own bowl by adding noodles, ackee mixture, and desired garnishes.
For an added touch, serve the Bun Bo Hue in a large, steaming hot pot, surrounded by an assortment of garnishes, such as lime wedges, chopped cilantro, and sliced chili peppers.
